title: L2 Tech Support Engineer
description:
Dear Technology Lover!

We're a small (but VERY efficient) 20-year-old MSP based in Arizona.

As a small company. we're VERY employee-focused, and believe that our tea
mbers are our MOST IMPORTANT ASSET!



Here are some of the things you can expect from working with us:

• Ongoing (monthly) manager review and feedback- you should never guess where you are "standing" and what you, AND THE COMPANY, can do better!

• Paid certifications- we will pay for your courses, and tests and allocate time during business hours to help you achieve your desired goals

• Paid Time Off- it's important to us that you are happy, healthy, and have a work-life balance!

• Bonuses- when the company wins- you win!

• Raises- As we grow, we believe you should as well. We have to stay competitive as an employer to retain our awesome employees!

• Opportunities to grow- In our industry, we can't be on "cruise control"... We need to push our tea
mbers to stay ahead of the technology curve. Help you continually to sharpen your skills with a focus on your career aspirations.

• Paid sick days- We understand life happens. We don't want you to feel that you have to drag yourself to work sick if your body tells you to relax...



You're the right fit if you:

• LOVE learning, working with, and experimenting with new technology

• Get excited when you save someone's day and provide great service

• Are a hard-working fun person, and see yourself being in the IT industry for the foreseeable future

• Want to work in a GROWING environment, with STABLE income, Bonuses, and a great attitude!

• Want the opportunity to advance and learn more!



Some of the responsibilities that your position will include are:

Helpdesk (mostly escalations, but some basic if needed), cyber security related tasks/ audits, cloud hosting and virtualization (HyperV, SonicWall, VPN), MS365 management and automation, BCDR (Axcient), networking (Unifi, Sonicwall), IT projects, Servers maintenance (Windows only), scripts creation, testing new technology and other tasks related to IT.



IMPORTANT:

This position requires AT LEAST 2 years of MSP experience and at least 2 years experience in an L2 position, supervisory, or lead role. 100% clean background check, highly organized, technologically savvy, and can work diligently with little 
veral hiring steps will take place to verify the above requirements, including reference checking, employment verification, technical testing, and more.



Minimum technical level that you will need to be able to pass a test on:

• Explaining to a non-technical person what the "cloud" is

• Consulting with a new client on what BCDR solution you would recommend to an SMB, and why

• Explaining what the differences are between traditional anti-virus and XDR

• Creating a new Virtual Machine on Hyper-V host, from an ISO file located on a different computer on the network.

• Explaining the differences and commonalities between SharePoint and OneDrive.

• Creating an Intune policy that will install Firefox on an Azure-joined VM, and make Firefox the default browser, with the specific home page we'll provide to you during the test.

• Several other situational and technical questions.

• CCNA certificate candidates preferred but not required.



MORE DETAILS:

• A PRE-EMPLOYMENT BACKGROUND CHECK is performed (at our expense). You must have 100% clean background check. Please do not apply if you don't have 100% clean background check.

• Personality is KEY for us. We are looking for an easy-going, yet professional, friendly and HONEST person.

• You will be working from home. This means you must have a quiet work environment and no excessive background noise.

• FAST AND RELIABLE INTERNET SERVICE IS REQUIRED With at least 100Mbps download speed  20+Mbps upload speed.

• As part of this position requirement, you must have a computer that will be used exclusively for your work with us. This means we will be installing our security software on that computer, will remove admin rights from you, and monitor/ control it for security and compliance reasons. In return, we will pay you additional “rent” fees, on top of your salary, in the amount of several hundred dollars. Details about this “rental” will be discussed during your second interview. There are minimum requirements that your computer must have, in order to be employed with us. You must have a Computer/Laptop with at least i5 minimum 9th gen processor (preferably a newer one) with 16 GB RAM or more or Ryzen 5/7 (MUST include TPM support) as well as Windows 11 OS.

• You must have recently worked on a night shift for 2 consecutive years or more.

• We evaluate/ review employees monthly and provide consistent feedback.

• We offer raises and bonuses according to performance, certifications, attitude, and customer reviews twice/ year.

• Your manager (our IT director) will create a roadmap for your "KPIs" and certifications track. We always push our team to earn certifications and additional knowledge – even allowing “disconnect study time” during business hours!

• On-call rotation (after-hours) for a week at a time, once every 4-5 weeks. It's VERY rare that we get calls after-hours (the average is only twice/ year!).

• We're closed on major US holidays, and at the completion of the first 3 months of employment, you'll qualify for paid holidays.

• Work hours are Mon-Fri at 8 am till 5:30 pm (UTC-07:00 , MST, Arizona time zone)

• Every 4 to 5 weeks, you'll be working 1/2 day on a Saturday (project work/catch-up work) and get 1/2 day off the week after

• Mandatory 1-hour lunch and additional breaks (as you need those) throughout the day.

• Further details and information will be discussed with qualified candidates.



EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:

• REQUIRED: Relevant minimum 2 YEARS experience working for an MSP

• REQUIRED: With AT LEAST 2 years experience in an L2 position, supervisory or lead role.

• REQUIRED: Microsoft Azure AD/ M365 experience

• REQUIRED: Experience working with an RMM and PSA software (DATTO preferred)

• Preferred: Cyber Security education/experience

• Preferred: Hyper-V experience



Other tools we use:

Barracuda DNS filter, AutoElevate, Cylance, Infocyte, Syxsense, IT Glue, SonicWall, Unifi, and more



HOW TO APPLY?



Answer the questions below, or you may also send an
 to 
 with the subject line "L2 Tech Support - Feb2026" with your answers:



- An attachment of your resume in PDF format/ link of you resume if you will send us a message.

- A confirmation (simple written statement) that you have a 100% clean background with no criminal history.

- A confirmation (simple written statement) that you understand the business hours (MST\ Arizona time zone.



Answer the following questions:

1. How many years of experience do you have working in an MSP company? Name of the MSP company/s you worked with.

2. Do you have at least 2 years experience in an L2 position, supervisory or lead role?

3. Do you have experience working with RMM and PSA software? What applications did you use?

4. Do you have experience working with Virtualization, servers, GPO, TCP/IP, hyper-v/vmware, conditional access, Intune, and Scripting such as PowerShell?

5. Have you recently worked on a night shift for 2 consecutive years or more? What time zone and when?

6. Are you okay with the computer requirements and the rental arrangement described in the job details?

7. Do you have an internet speed of at least 100Mbps download speed and  20+ upload speed?



We're looking forward to having you join our family!

title: Operations Lead / Project Manager (Second Brain to Founder)
description:
Read the full post for application instructions.



*Location: Remote (Philippines-based)



*Role Overview:



We are hiring a high-level Operations Lead / Project Manager to work directly with the founder of a fast-growing coaching and speaking business.



This role is not task-based. It is ownership-based.



You will be responsible for organizing, coordinating, and driving execution across multiple areas including:



- speaking engagements

- client operations

- content and social media

- scheduling and logistics

- day-to-day business operations



Your primary function is to ensure that nothing falls through the cracks and that the founder’s world runs smoothly, clearly, and efficiently.



*Who This Role Is For



This role is for someone who:

- naturally takes ownership

- thinks ahead without being told

- is calm under pressure

- can manage complexity and shifting priorities

- is highly organized but also adaptable

- is comfortable working with a fast-moving, big-picture, emotionally intelligent founder



*Who This Role Is NOT For



- people who wait for instructions

- people who only complete assigned tasks

- people who need constant supervision

- people who struggle with ambiguity

- people who avoid follow-up or confrontation

- people who are rigid and cannot adapt to a dynamic environment



*Key Responsibilities



1. Calendar & Scheduling Ownership

- Fully manage and own the founder’s calendar

- Ensure all appointments are confirmed, complete, and accurate

- Anticipate conflicts, fatigue, and logistical issues

- Prepare the founder for all commitments



2. Project & Task Management

- Maintain a central system for all tasks and projects

- Assign clear owners, deadlines, and priorities

- Track progress daily and follow through until completion

- Ensure nothing is dropped across people or timelines



3. Operations Coordination

- Coordinate across tea
mbers (content, editor, assistant, etc.)

- Manage workflows and ensure deadlines are met

- Identify bottlenecks and resolve them proactively



4. Speaking & Business Operations

- Manage speaking pipeline from inquiry to delivery

- Coordinate contracts, logistics, prep, and follow-ups

- Support business development and client experience



5. Content & Social Media Operations

- Oversee content pipeline (planning, editing, posting)

- Ensure consistency and execution across platforms

- Coordinate with editors and content creators



6. Reporting & Communication

- Provide clear, structured daily and weekly updates

- Surface risks and blockers early

- Communicate in a way that reduces decision fatigue



*Core Competencies



Ownership

- You take responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks.



Anticipation

- You think 2–3 steps ahead and prevent problems before they happen.



Follow-through

- You do not leave things half-done. You close loops completely.



Systems Thinking

- You can create and manage systems that keep everything organized and visible.



Emotional Intelligence

- You can work with a fast-paced, expressive founder without becoming reactive or overwhelmed.



Adaptability

- You can operate in a dynamic environment where priorities shift.



*Experience Requirements

- Minimum 5+ years in operations, project management, or executive support

- Experience working with founders, executives, or high-level leaders

- Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

- Experience coordinating across teams and functions

- Strong organizational and communication skills



Preferred:

- Master’s degree in Business, Management, or related field

- Experience in coaching, consulting, media, or personal brand businesses



*Tools & Systems



Experience with tools such as:

- Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Calendar)

- Notion, Asana, ClickUp, or similar

- Slack / WhatsApp communication

- Project tracking systems



Tool flexibility is important. Thinking matters more than tools.



*Work Style Requirements

- Highly responsive and reliable during working hours

- Comfortable with real-time communication (WhatsApp, voice notes, etc.)

- Able to translate fast, unstructured input into clear action

- Able to handle pressure without becoming reactive



*What Success Looks Like

- The founder feels less overwhelmed

- Tasks and projects move without being chased

- Communication becomes clearer and more structured

- Fewer mistakes and dropped details

- The business operates more smoothly and predictably



*Compensation

- Competitive for Philippines-based roles

- Based on experience and level of ownership

- Includes opportunity to grow into a full-time leadership role



*Probationary Period

- Initial 45–60 day trial period (part-time)

- Clear performance expectations

- Opportunity to transition into a full-time role based on performance



*Final Note



This is a high-trust, high-responsibility role.



You are not here to assist.

You are here to operate, organize, and drive execution.



If you are someone who takes ownership, thinks deeply, and wants to be part of building something meaningful, we would love to hear from you.



Do not apply unless you meet the experience and thinking standards outlined above.



This is a high-responsibility role that requires strong ownership, anticipation, and the ability to operate without constant direction. We are not looking for entry-level applicants or task-based assistants.



Compensation is flexible for the right person and level of responsibility. We are open to structuring this role at a higher level if there is strong alignment.



All candidates will begin with a part-time trial period (45–60 days) to assess real working fit. High performers will have the opportunity to transition quickly into a full-time, long-term role with increased responsibility and compensation.



I will not be reviewing applications here. Please send your resume, portfolio, and a thoughtful cover letter to amina [at] zamanicoachinggroup [dot] com. Only complete applications will be considered.



Use the exact subject line:

Project Manager Application – [Full Name] – [Years of Experience]

title: YouTube Video Editor
description:
I’m looking for a skilled video editor to help produce 8 videos per month (2 per week) for my personal brand/business content.



Pay: $50 USD per video + additional $50/video bonus if it passes 2,000 views in it's first week of posting.



Volume: Consistent ongoing work



Turnaround: Fast turnaround is very important



What You’ll Be Editing:



I will provide (1) Raw recorded video and (2) the script I used



Your job is to turn it into an engaging finished video.



Responsibilities:

- Clean up audio and video so it follows the script

- Remove mistakes, pauses, filler words, dead space, etc.

- Tighten pacing for retention

- Add zoom ins / zoom outs where helpful

- Add relevant b-roll, clips, 
s, movie scenes, reaction 
nts, or visuals when appropriate

- Keep the video dynamic and engaging throughout

- Add English captions (I will give you the script)

- Deliver final polished video ready to publish

- Create two engaging thumbnail options for each video



This is the style of video I'm looking for: 



What I’m Looking For:

- Strong sense of pacing and viewer retention

- Knows how to make talking-head content engaging

- Understands YouTube style editing

- Can choose visuals that improve the story, not distract from it

- Reliable and responsive

- Can meet deadlines consistently

- Good English comprehension



Important:



I need someone who responds fast and can work quickly without sacrificing quality. If you are slow, this will not be a fit.



To Apply, please send:



1. Examples of YouTube videos you edited

2. Why you think you’d be a good fit

3. The word 
ntum in your application so I know you read this



Long-term opportunity for the right person.

title: Senior Ads Manager
description:
Role Overview



We are hiring a Senior Performance Ads Manager who takes full ownership of ad accounts — not just execution.



This is not a “set up campaigns and send reports” role.



You will be responsible for performance, stability, and recovery across multiple client accounts.



You should be comfortable operating in high-pressure environments, where accounts scale fast, get flagged, or require rapid restructuring.



What You Will Own



End-to-end management of Meta 
/Instagram) and Google Ads accounts

Budget ownership ranging from $10,000 to $100,000+ per month

Campaign planning, launch, testing, scaling, and controlled shutdowns

Diagnosing performance drops and fixing them without hand-holding

Working closely with creative, tracking, and operations teams

Handling policy issues, disapprovals, and account restrictions

Clear weekly performance reporting focused on decisions, not dashboards

Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



You must have:



3+ years of hands-on ad buying experience (Meta required, Google preferred)

Personally managed at least $10,000/month in ad spend

Strong understanding of:

Conversion tracking (pixels, CAPI, GA4, GTM)

Attribution trade-offs and data reliability

Experience working with multiple clients simultaneously

Clear written English (you will be client-facing)

If you have only worked on:



boosted posts

single internal brands

learning projects

Do not apply.



Strong Advantage (Read Carefully)



You will be prioritized if you have experience with:



Restricted or sensitive verticals (e.g. gambling, crypto, health, IPTV, finance)

Account bans, BM restrictions, or repeated disapprovals

Pre-emptive compliance structuring and risk isolation

Ad account warm-ups and scaling under constraints

You do not need to be a “blackhat”, but you must understand how platforms actually behave in the real world.



How We Measure You



CPA / ROAS stability over time

Ability to diagnose problems before they become disasters

Quality of decisions under pressure

Clarity in communication with internal teams and management

Application Question (Mandatory)



Answer this clearly and honestly:



“Describe the worst ad account failure or ban you have handled.What happened, and what exactly did you do in the first 72 hours?”



Vague or generic answers will be rejected automatically.



What This Role Is NOT



Not a junior execution role

Not a learning position

Not a reporting-only job

Not suitable if you need step-by-step instructions

Summary



If you are an ads manager who:



owns outcomes,

understands risk,

and can think under pressure,

this role will suit you.



If you are looking to “learn ads” or follow SOPs, this role will not be a fit.



Job Type: Full-time



Benefits:





Flexible schedule

Work from home

Experience:





 Advertising: 3 years (Required)

Work Location: Remote

title: Ad Manager (Performance Marketing | Meta & Google)
description:
We’re looking for a data-driven Ad Manager to manage client ad accounts across Meta, Google, and other paid platforms.

The goal is simple — to run profitable campaigns, hit performance targets, and communicate results clearly.



You’ll work with the creative and operations team to strategize, launch, and scale campaigns for multiple clients across various niches — including restricted categories such as pharma, gambling, IPTV, or crypto (experience in these is a plus but not mandatory).



?



Key Responsibilities

 • Plan, launch, and optimize ad campaigns on Meta 
/Instagram) and Google Ads.

 • Manage multiple client accounts, budgets, and daily performance targets.

 • Write and test ad copy, headlines, and creatives in coordination with the design team.

 • Monitor KPIs (CTR, CPC, CPL, CPA, ROAS) and optimize accordingly.

 • Prepare weekly performance reports and insights for management.

 • Collaborate with the creative and tracking teams to test new funnels, angles, and audiences.

 • Stay updated with ad policies, bans, and platform workarounds (knowledge of cloaking, warm-ups, and compliance is a big plus).



?



Requirements

 • 2+ years of hands-on ad buying experience (Meta or Google essential; both preferred).

 • Proven track record of managing at least $10,000/month in ad spend profitably.

 • Strong understanding of tracking, attribution, pixels, and conversion APIs.

 • Ability to analyze data and make decisions based on numbers, not intuition.

 • Excellent written English and communication — you’ll be client-facing.

 • Comfortable working with international clients and in IST time zone (3 PM – 11 PM).

 • Bonus: experience with restricted or high-risk verticals and ability to adapt quickly when accounts are flagged or disabled.



?



???? Compensation

 • Full-time (40 hrs/week)

 • $800 – $1,200 USD/month, depending on experience

 • Monthly performance bonus for hitting ROAS or CPA targets

 • Paid trial for shortlisted candidates



?



?? Filter Question (Required)



Please start your application by answering this in one line:



“You launch a campaign that spends $200 but gets only 2 leads — what’s the first thing you check and why?”



(Answer in under 30 seconds — this helps us see how you think analytically.)



Applications without this answer will not be considered.



?



???? Optional (for strong candidates)



Include screenshots or brief examples of your previous ad account results — even partial ones (blurred budgets are fine).

title: Full-Time Ads Manager
description:
We’re hiring a full-time Ads Manager to join our high-performance marketing team. This role involves end-to-end campaign management across some of the most challenging and high-ROI verticals online.



???? Key Responsibilities

 • Campaign Setup & Launch: Execute campaign builds across Meta, TikTok, Taboola, and other platforms. You’ll set targeting, upload creatives, and ensure proper tracking is in place.

 • Ongoing Optimization: Daily monitoring and performance tuning to improve Return on Ad Spend (ROAS), reduce CPA, and maintain ad account health.

 • Reporting & Documentation: Maintain internal sheets and reporting dashboards. Submit weekly performance reports with insights, next steps, and targets.

 • Task & Time Management: Use Asana to stay on top of deadlines, track progress, and update stakeholders regularly.

 • Client & Vendor Coordination: Communicate with clients, creative vendors, and account suppliers via
, Slack, or WhatsApp. Ensure updates are acknowledged and implemented swiftly.

 • Responsiveness: Acknowledge and act on tasks and messages promptly during working hours.

 • Issue Escalation: Flag delays, ad rejections, or campaign blockers early to leadership with potential solutions.

 • Consistent Attention to Detail: Campaigns run 24/7 — a quick response mindset and proactive follow-through are key.



???? Preferred Experience

 • 1+ years running ads in restricted niches such as crypto, gambling, cannabis, forex, or similar.

 • Familiarity with cloaking, tracking setups (CAPI, S2S), or alternate ad account vendors is a plus.

 • Solid understanding of Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Ads, and native platforms like Taboola.



? What We’re Looking For

 • Independent operator who doesn’t need hand-holding.

 • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 • Strong organizational skills and ownership mindset.

 • Comfortable working remotely and coordinating across time zones.

title: Takeoff Technician (Junior – PlanSwift)
description:
Blitz Contractor Solutions is looking for a Junior Takeoff Technician to support our estimating team. This role is focused on quantity takeoffs using PlanSwift and is ideal for someone early in their career with exposure to US and Canada construction projects.



Role Overview

You will be responsible for completing accurate quantity takeoffs based on project drawings. You’ll work within a structured workflow with guidance from senior estimators. This is a production-focused role requiring consistency, accuracy, and attention to detail.



Key Responsibilities

- Perform quantity takeoffs using PlanSwift

- Review drawings (architectural, structural, etc.) to define scope

- Extract and organize quantities (areas, lengths, counts, volumes)

- Label and structure takeoff outputs clearly

- Follow established templates and workflows

- Revise takeoffs based on feedback from senior estimators



Skills & Requirements

- Basic experience using PlanSwift (required)

- Exposure to US and/or Canada construction projects

- Ability to read and navigate construction drawings

- Can perform basic calculations (areas, volumes, counts, conversions)

- Background in engineering, architecture, or related field

- Strong attention to detail and accuracy

- Willingness to learn and follow structured processes

- Good communication and responsiveness



Work Setup

- Full-time role requiring dedicated availability

- Candidates must not be engaged in other full-time or concurrent roles during working hours

- Can be work from home, or on-site in Sta. Maria, Bulacan for candidates within proximity

- Application Requirement (Mandatory)



To be considered, candidates must submit sample takeoffs completed in PlanSwift.



Submission must include:

- Marked-up drawings or screenshots showing takeoff work

- Clear quantity outputs (areas, lengths, counts, etc.)

- Organized breakdown by scope or trade



Guidelines:

- Must be your own work

- Preferably from US or Canada projects

- Carpentry, finishes, or general trades preferred



Applications without a sample takeoff will not be reviewed. Submissions that are unclear, incomplete, or poorly structured will not be considered.

title: Legal Assistant
description:
Job Title: Legal Assistant – Personal Injury / Criminal Law Firm (Remote)



The Hatton Law Firm PLLC is seeking a dependable, organized, and experienced Legal Assistant to join our growing remote team. We are a Houston, Texas based law firm handling both Personal Injury and Criminal Defense matters.



We are looking for someone with prior law firm experience who understands legal workflows, case management systems, client communication, and file organization. This is a mid-level role supporting attorneys and staff while helping keep cases organized and moving forward.



Position Type:



* Full-Time

* Remote

* Monday through Friday

* 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM Central Standard Time (Houston, Texas USA)



Responsibilities:



* Audit case files in the system for missing documents, notes, and deadlines

* Review files for accuracy and completeness

* Update client records and case notes

* Assist with personal injury and criminal case workflows

* Upload, organize, and maintain digital files

* Draft letters,
s, and basic legal documents

* Communicate with clients regarding status updates and missing items

* Coordinate with providers, courts, or outside offices as directed

* Manage calendars, reminders, and task lists

* Support attorneys and case managers with daily operations



Required Experience:



* Prior experience working in a law firm

* Some experience in Personal Injury cases required

* Criminal law experience is a plus

* Strong administrative and organizational skills

* Experience auditing files and maintaining accurate records

* Ability to manage multiple matters at once



Legal Software Experience Required:



Must have experience using one or more of the following:



* Filevine

* Clio

* CASEpeer



Requirements:



* Excellent English written and verbal communication

* Strong attention to detail

* Professional phone and
 etiquette

* Reliable high-speed internet

* Quiet professional workspace

* Able to work during Houston business hours

* Self-starter with strong follow-through



Compensation:



Based on experience and qualifications.



To Apply:



Please send:



1. Resume

2. Short introduction

3. Law firm experience

4. Personal injury experience

5. Which legal software you have used

7. Why you are a good fit for this position



We are looking for someone reliable, sharp, detail-oriented, and ready to grow long-term with our firm.

title: Creative Quality Manager
description:
ROLE SUMMARY

We are looking for a highly perceptive, creatively sharp Creative Quality Manager to help strengthen the quality, consistency, and scalability of our photo and video delivery pipeline. This person will serve as a high-level internal creative checkpoint — reviewing deliverables, identifying recurring quality issues, spotting patterns across shooters and editors, and helping our team improve creative consistency without adding more burden to already-stretched internal leaders. This is not a basic admin or upload role. We are looking for someone with a strong creative eye first, especially in video, who can also think operationally, document issues clearly, and help improve backend workflows over time.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

- Review video deliverables before final delivery to ensure quality and consistency

- Catch technical and creative misses before they reach the client

- Identify patterns across shooters and editors, including recurring strengths, misses, and coaching opportunities

- Flag issues related to coverage, pacing, framing, shot variety, detail shots, creative direction, music choice, captions, and overall polish

- Organize observations and feedback clearly for internal leadership

- Help create stronger quality control standards and backend review processes

- Support delivery readiness by ensuring projects are complete, clean, and aligned with internal expectations

- Help reduce the amount of proofing, review, and repetitive delivery work sitting on senior internal tea
mbers

- Contribute to SOPs, workflow improvements, and quality documentation as the team grows

- Writing feedback briefs to photographers and videographers regarding review and delivery

- Ensuring brand standards for photos and videos (correct logos, fonts, colors, etc.)



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

- Fewer quality issues slipping through to clients

- Better visibility into shooter/editor performance trends

- Stronger internal feedback loops for creative coaching

- More consistency across delivered video content

- Less manual review burden on the internal team

- Cleaner backend systems and a more scalable delivery process



IDEAL BACKGROUND

- Strong video editing or post-production background

- Excellent creative judgment and attention to detail

- Ability to spot both technical and aesthetic issues quickly

- Experience reviewing work for quality, not just producing it

- Organized, process-aware, and able to document findings clearly

- Real estate media experience

- Comfortable working behind the scenes in a support and oversight capacity

- Strong AI skillsets such as Higgsfield, Nano Banana Pro, Eleven Labs, Claude, and others



NICE TO HAVE

- Experience working in a fast-paced, high-volume creative environment

- Familiarity with distributed editing teams or offshore creative support

- Operational or workflow improvement experience

- Experience helping train, guide, or support photographers/editors



THIS ROLE IS NOT

- A client-facing phone support role

- A purely admin or upload-only VA role

- A replacement for creative leadership

- A basic checklist-only QC seat



This role is for someone who can combine creative discernment, consistency, and backend support to help level up our internal quality control and creative execution.



WHO YOU ARE

You have a strong eye, high standards, and good taste. You notice what others miss. You care about the difference between “technically complete” and “actually good.” You can bring structure to creative work without draining the humanity out of it. You help teams get better, not just busier.

title: Client Care Specialist
description:
MISSION

The Client Care Specialist is an operational admin role and is the relationship hub— this role ensures agents feel taken care of, the calendar is protected, and opportunities for growth are identified daily. You manage client communication, control scheduling, support light sales, and strengthen long-term relationships. When active clients are fully supported, you shift into outreach and revenue-building mode.



DAILY SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

- Agents feel supported and confident

- Calendar is clean, optimized, and protected

- Sales opportunities are identified and surfaced

- Relationships are actively maintained

- No client is left wondering what is going on



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES



1. Client Communication and Care

- Monitor and respond to
, text, Slack, and CR
ssages

- Maintain a friendly, fast, concierge-level tone

- Confidently answer questions about pricing, packages, and services

- Recommend upgrades and add-ons when appropriate

- Resolve small issues independently

- Prioritize long-term and OG clients

- Log notes and important details in the CRM

- Agents should feel like they have a personal point of contact, not a ticket system.



2. Scheduling and Calendar Control

- Fully manage the calendar inside Tonomo

- Confirm appointments and resolve scheduling questions

- Optimize routes and timing for efficiency

- Push back on unreasonable requests using solution-first language

- Avoid saying no when possible by presenting options

- Decline same-day requests if they hurt operations

- Escalate truly critical situations to Tyler

- Protect weekday scheduling and limit weekends unless approved

- The calendar exists to support quality, not chaos.



3. Light Sales Support and Opportunity Spotting

- This role supports growth without pressure selling.

- Upsell add-ons when agents ask questions

- Suggest listing campaigns, Zillow Showcase, video, or premium services when relevant

- Identify high-intent or hot agents

- Flag those agents for Manager to personally follow up

- Support value-based conversations, not discounts



4. Client Outreach and Relationship Building

- Once active tasks are complete, shift into outreach mode.

- Reach out to agents who have not ordered recently

- Send friendly check-in texts,
s, or calls

- Reconnect with past clients to stay top of mind

- Perform care calls after projects are delivered

- Ensure clients are happy and heard

- Ask for reviews when expectations are met

- Log outreach and results in the CRM



5. Care Call Guidelines

- Phone calls are preferred when possible

- Text is acceptable and encouraged when appropriate

- If an agent has multiple jobs in a short time, one phone call per week is sufficient

- Follow-up texts can be used to check in and confirm satisfaction

- Dealer’s choice, based on context and relationship

- Goal is connection, not interruption



6. Review Requests

- Review asks typically happen during care calls

- Text follow-ups are also acceptable

- If satisfaction is confirmed, politely ask for a Google review

- Track reviews received

- Thank agents for their time and support

- Reviews are a growth lever. Treat them with intention.



7. Project Awareness and Support

- While evening ops owns most delivery and QC:

- Monitor Tonomo for overnight deliveries

- Handle morning video deliveries if they come in

- Assist with follow-ups tied to completed jobs

- Coordinate with evening ops when questions arise



TOOLS USED DAILY

- Tonomo for scheduling, orders, delivery, and invoicing

- Slack for internal communication

-
 and text for client communication

- GoHighLevel CRM for notes, outreach, and tracking

- Social media is not part of this role\



TYPICAL HOURS

- Start time between 8:30 and 9:00 AM (US)

- End time 5:00 PM

title: Procurement & Architectural Finishes Coordinator (experience with US-based client required) | Competitive Pay Awaits
description:
Competitive Pay | Growth Opportunity | Work with U.S. Real Estate Company



About Us

We are a fast-growing U.S.-based real estate development company focused on high-quality home renovations and new construction projects in California.



We don’t just build homes — we build efficient systems, strong teams, and scalable operations.



We’re looking for a detail-oriented, proactive, and reliable tea
mber who can help us manage finishes, materials, and procurement across multiple projects.

______________________________________________________



Why This Role is Important

This role is the backbone of our construction operations. If materials are late, wrong, or missing, projects get delayed.



Your job is to make sure that NEVER happens.

______________________________________________________



What You’ll Be Doing

Finish & Plan Coordination

     -Review architectural drawings and finish schedules

     -Identify required materials (flooring, tiles, cabinets, fixtures, lighting, appliances)

     -Help recommend finish options when needed



Procurement & Purchasing

     -Request and compare vendor quotes

     -Analyze pricing, availability, and lead times

     -Create purchase orders

     -Confirm orders with vendors



Delivery & Logistics Tracking

     -Track all material deliveries and shipments

     -Monitor lead times and backorders

     -Coordinate delivery schedules with project timelines



Project Support

     -Maintain procurement tracking sheets (Google Sheets / Excel)

     -Coordinate with project managers, vendors, and team

     -Proactively flag risks and delays (VERY IMPORTANT)



What We’re Looking For

Required

     -Degree in Architecture, Interior Design, Construction, or related field

     -At least 2+ years experience working with construction drawings or projects

     -Strong ability to read plans (floor plans, elevations, finish schedules, etc.)

     -Highly organized and detail-oriented

     -Strong English communication (written & verbal)



Preferred (Big Plus)

     -Experience in U.S. residential construction

     -Knowledge of materials (tile, flooring, cabinets, plumbing fixtures, lighting)

     -Experience sourcing or purchasing construction materials



Tools You May Use

     -AutoCAD / Revit

     -SketchUp

     -Bluebeam

     -Google Sheets / Excel



How to Apply (IMPORTANT)

To be considered, submit:

     -Your resume

     -A short summary of your experience with construction drawings

     -Example project(s) you’ve worked on

     -Photo of your workstation

     -screenshot of your device specifications

     -screenshot of your internet speed test results
